children.heading: Studies on introduction of aquatic nuisance species by vessels
children.label: a Studies on introduction of aquatic nuisance species by vessels
A
children.text: to assess the environmental effects of ballast water exchange on the diversity and abundance of native species in receiving estuarine, marine, and fresh waters of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: to identify areas within the waters of the United States and the exclusive economic zone, if any, where the exchange of ballast water does not pose a threat of infestation or spread of aquatic nuisance species in the Great Lakes and other waters of the United States.

children.heading: Biological study
children.label: 2 Biological study
children.text: The Task Force, in cooperation with the Secretary, shall conduct a study to determine whether aquatic nuisance species threaten the ecological characteristics and economic uses of Lake Champlain and other waters of the United States other than the Great Lakes.

children.label: A
children.text: the degree to which shipping may be a major pathway of transmission of aquatic nuisance species in those waters;

children.label: B
children.text: possible alternatives for controlling introduction of those species through shipping; and

children.label: C
children.text: the feasibility of implementing regional versus national control measures.

children.heading: Ecological and ballast water discharge surveys
children.label: b Ecological and ballast water discharge surveys
i
children.text: to be highly susceptible to invasion by aquatic nuisance species resulting from ballast water operations and other operations of vessels; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to require further study.

children.label: i
children.text: examine the attributes and patterns of invasions of aquatic nuisance species; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide an estimate of the effectiveness of ballast water management and other vessel management guidelines issued and regulations promulgated under this subchapter in abating invasions of aquatic nuisance species in the waters that are the subject of the survey.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in cooperation with the Task Force, shall conduct surveys of ballast water discharge rates and practices in the waters referred to in paragraph (1)(A) on the basis of the criteria under clauses (i) and (ii) of such paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: examine the rate of, and trends in, ballast water discharge in the waters that are the subject of the survey; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assess the effectiveness of voluntary guidelines issued, and regulations promulgated, under this subchapter in altering ballast water discharge practices to reduce the probability of accidental introductions of aquatic nuisance species.

children.heading: Columbia River
children.label: 3 Columbia River
children.text: The Secretary, in cooperation with the Task Force and academic institutions in each of the States affected, shall conduct an ecological and ballast water discharge survey of the Columbia River system consistent with the requirements of paragraphs (1) and (2).

children.heading: Ballast exchange
children.label: 1 Ballast exchange
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , and prior to the effective date of the regulations issued under (as in effect on the day before ), the Task Force shall submit a report to the Congress that presents the results of the study required under subsection (a)(1) and makes recommendations with respect to such regulations.November 29, 1990December 4, 2018section 4711(b) of this title

children.heading: Biological and shipping studies
children.label: 2 Biological and shipping studies
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Secretary and the Task Force shall each submit to the Congress a report on the results of their respective studies under paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (a).November 29, 1990

children.heading: Negotiations
children.label: d Negotiations
children.text: The Secretary, working through the International Maritime Organization, is encouraged to enter into negotiations with the governments of foreign countries concerning the planning and implementation of measures aimed at the prevention and control of unintentional introductions of aquatic nuisance species in coastal waters.

make available not to exceed $750,000 to fund research on aquatic nuisance species prevention and control in the Chesapeake Bay through grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, to universities and research institutions;

make available not to exceed $500,000 to fund research on aquatic nuisance species prevention and control in the Gulf of Mexico through grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, to universities and research institutions;

make available not to exceed $500,000 to fund research on aquatic nuisance species prevention and control for the Pacific Coast through grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, to universities and research institutions;

make available not to exceed $500,000 to fund research on aquatic nuisance species prevention and control for the Atlantic Coast through grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, to universities and research institutions; and

make available not to exceed $750,000 to fund research on aquatic nuisance species prevention and control in the San Francisco Bay-Delta Estuary through grants, to be competitively awarded and subject to peer review, to universities and research institutions.

children.label: A
children.text: ballasting practices;

children.label: B
children.text: compliance with the guidelines issued pursuant to (as in effect on the day before ); andsection 4711(c) of this titleDecember 4, 2018

children.label: C
children.text: any other information obtained by the Task Force under subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The owner or operator of a vessel subject to this chapter shall submit to the National Ballast Information Clearinghouse, by not later than 6 hours after the arrival of the vessel at a United States port or place of destination, the ballast water management report form approved by the Office of Management and Budget numbered OMB 1625–0069 (or a successor form), unless the vessel is operating exclusively on a voyage between ports or places within contiguous portions of a single Captain of the Port Zone.

children.heading: Multiple discharges
children.label: B Multiple discharges
children.text: The owner or operator of a vessel subject to this chapter may submit a single report under subparagraph (A) for multiple ballast water discharges within a single port or place of destination during the same voyage.

children.label: i
children.text: not later than 24 hours prior to arrival at a United States port or place of destination in the State, if the voyage of the vessel is anticipated to exceed 24 hours; or

children.label: ii
children.text: before departing the port or place of departure, if the voyage of the vessel to the United States port or place of destination is not anticipated to exceed 24 hours.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a form submitted electronically, immediately disseminate the report to interested States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a form submitted by means other than electronically, disseminate the report to interested States as soon as practicable.

children.heading: Availability to public
children.label: B Availability to public
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of a ballast water management report under paragraph (2), the National Ballast Information Clearinghouse shall make the data in the report fully and readily available to the public in a searchable and fully retrievable electronic format.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall prepare and submit a report in accordance with this paragraph.July 1, 2019

children.label: i
children.text: ballast water delivery and management; and

children.label: ii
children.text: invasions of aquatic nuisance species resulting from ballast water.

children.label: i
children.text: the Task Force; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Smithsonian Institution (acting through the Smithsonian Environmental Research Center).

children.label: I
children.text: the Task Force;

children.label: II
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make each report available to the public.

children.heading: Working group
children.label: 5 Working group
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish a working group, including members from the National Ballast Information Clearinghouse and States with ballast water management programs, to establish a process for compiling and readily sharing Federal and State commercial vessel reporting and enforcement data regarding compliance with this chapter.December 4, 2018
